hero-tagline: I design reliable data products, analytical workflows, and BI solutions that transform complex financial and regulatory data into actionable business decisions.
about-who-p1: I'm Tristan, a Data & Business Analyst working at the interface between business teams, risk functions, and information systems, across regulated banking and international industrial environments. Over four years I have owned delivery end to end: from requirements and stakeholder workshops to data pipelines, dashboards, and production rollout.
about-who-p2: I'm currently on a mission within the automotive-leasing subsidiary of a major European banking group, where I own regulatory delivery on Basel III Standard Approach frameworks, NDoD reporting, and credit risk segmentation. Engineering degree from Télécom Saint-Étienne, FRM certification in progress (GARP). Based in Bordeaux, open to full-remote roles across Western Europe.
about-value-1: Translate business requirements into data solutions
experience-1-title: Data & Business Analyst · Risk & Finance IT
experience-1-employer: Solutec, on mission at the automotive-leasing subsidiary of a major European banking group
experience-1-bullet1: Owned the design and delivery of an Oracle SQL processing engine covering default-event detection, cure identification, and prudential portfolio segmentation. Translated complex regulatory logic (NDoD, EBA / Basel III / CRR) into auditable data transformations, through to production.
experience-1-bullet2: Led requirements across Risk, Finance, and Compliance, and authored the functional specifications (business rules, data dictionaries, ETL flows, test cases) that became the team's reference.
experience-1-bullet3: Facilitated scoping and prioritisation workshops as MOA / MOE liaison, owned the Jira backlog, and drove user stories from specification to production.
experience-1-bullet4: Coordinated UAT campaigns end to end, owning anomaly traceability and the correction loop.
experience-1-bullet5: Built credit-risk KPI monitoring and data-quality controls, and reported delivery and risk status to the steering committee.
experience-2-employer: Lennox EMEA, listed industrial group, EMEA scope
experience-2-bullet1: Owned an end-to-end BI migration (legacy to Power BI / SSRS) across European entities: requirements, architecture, deployment, and user training, lifting cross-department reporting efficiency by 30%.
experience-2-bullet2: Built and shipped an XGBoost forecasting model into the S&OP process, and presented results and recommendations directly to business stakeholders, improving forecast reliability by 25%.
experience-2-bullet3: Designed Python / SQL pipelines automating multi-entity financial reporting flows.
experience-2-bullet4: Built cost and margin analytics that drove the quarterly removal of 10+ unprofitable projects, alongside dynamic production, sales, and finance dashboards.
projects-intro: Self-directed projects where I owned the full cycle: scoping, architecture, build, and delivery. Each one is framed as a business case: context, problem, solution, and outcome.
project-1-context: European banking institutions must classify credit exposures and calculate capital requirements under the Basel III Standard Approach using auditable, production-grade data pipelines.
project-1-problem: Risk and finance teams need a transparent, reproducible engine that turns raw exposure data into regulatory risk-weight assignments and RWA figures, without relying on opaque spreadsheets.
project-1-solution: Designed and built a PL/SQL processing engine on Oracle XE that segments exposures, assigns Basel III Standard Approach risk weights, computes RWA, and exposes reconciliation views through a Streamlit interface.
project-1-outcome: Delivered an auditable, end-to-end scoring pipeline producing regulator-ready risk-weight and capital figures, with full traceability from raw exposure to RWA.
project-1-screenshot-alt: Screenshot of the Basel III Credit Scoring Engine showing the credit loss distribution and capital threshold
project-2-context: Investors, regulators and risk teams need to compare the prudential health of major European banks using standardised capital and liquidity ratios.
project-2-problem: Public EBA disclosures are spread across multiple reports and formats, making it hard to compare CET1, Tier 1, LCR and NSFR ratios across institutions at a glance.
project-2-solution: Building a Power BI dashboard on public EBA data that consolidates prudential ratios into a single comparative view across major European banks.
project-2-outcome: A reusable, refreshable benchmark dashboard for comparing bank-level capital and liquidity positions against regulatory minimums.
project-2-screenshot-alt: Preview of the European Banks Regulatory Dashboard comparing prudential ratios across banks
project-3-context: Lenders adopting machine-learning credit models must be able to justify individual decisions to regulators and applicants under explainability requirements.
project-3-problem: Risk analysts need a credit scoring model whose predictions can be explained at the individual level, not just in aggregate.
project-3-solution: Built an explainable scoring engine using Python, XGBoost and SHAP: an end-to-end credit model on a synthetic loan portfolio, deployed as an interactive Streamlit app where users input borrower data and receive a score plus a feature-level breakdown.
project-3-outcome: Generated interpretable risk scores with transparent, feature-level explanations for every prediction, bridging ML performance with regulatory explainability needs.
project-3-screenshot-alt: Screenshot of the XGBoost credit scoring app showing SHAP feature contributions
